title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Poirot and Mrs. Oliver discuss Judith, a charming widow whose husband, a pilot, died in a car accident. Mrs. Oliver notes that Judith performs clerical work and has one child, Miranda. Poirot expresses interest in investigating the deceased and the missing, specifically a girl exchange student who may have been involved in legal trouble. 

Poirot shows Mrs. Oliver his notebook, which contains a "Death List" including the wealthy Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith and Miss Janet White, a school teacher and law firm employee who was killed with a knife and had a history of forgery. He also notes "Missing Girl's Cry" and the word "forgery" regarding a potential addition to a will that favored the exchange girl. Poirot mentions the word "elephant" might be connected to the case. He informs Mrs. Oliver that he has an appointment at the firm Fullerton, Harrison, and Readbate in Manchester to discuss the forgery and speak with people involved in the preparation process.

At the law firm, the senior partner, Mr. Jeremy Fullerton, receives Poirot, who is accompanied by Inspector Henry Largent and retired Inspector Spence. Fullerton recalls a past case where Spence had insisted the wrong man was convicted, a case eventually solved by a Belgian detective. Fullerton reflects on the murder of a child at a gala, noting he has a sharp idea regarding the killer but remains uncertain among three suspects: three idle young men.
